contentBlocks.title: Why convert JPG to PNG?
contentBlocks.html:
JPG (JPEG) is a lossy format optimised for photographs. Every time a JPG file is re-saved, the image loses a small amount of detail due to lossy compression. PNG, on the other hand, uses lossless compression — no data is discarded, so the image remains pixel-perfect.

Converting JPG to PNG is useful when you need to edit an image further without accumulating compression artefacts, when a platform requires PNG uploads, or when you want to add transparency later in a graphics editor.

Keep in mind that converting a JPG to PNG does not restore detail already lost during the original JPG compression. The resulting PNG will be a faithful, lossless copy of the JPG — but at a larger file size.

This tool processes everything locally in your browser. Your images are never uploaded to any server.

contentBlocks.title: When should you convert JPG to PNG?
contentBlocks.description: Common use cases for JPG-to-PNG conversion:

items.title: Further image editing
items.description: If you plan to edit a photo in a graphics tool, converting to PNG first prevents additional quality loss from repeated JPG saves.

items.title: Platform requirements
items.description: Some websites, print services, and submission portals specifically require PNG uploads. A quick conversion ensures compliance.

items.title: Adding transparency
items.description: PNG supports an alpha channel. Convert to PNG, then use a graphics editor to remove the background and save with transparency.

items.title: Print-quality archiving
items.description: Store important images in lossless PNG to prevent further degradation, especially if the original RAW file is unavailable.

contentBlocks.title: How image conversion impacts page speed and SEO
contentBlocks.html:
Core Web Vitals is a set of performance metrics Google uses when evaluating websites. One of them — LCP (Largest Contentful Paint) — measures the time it takes for the largest visible element to appear on screen. On many pages, that element is an image.

Converting JPG images to PNG reduces graphic file sizes, which directly shortens resource download time and improves the LCP score. Smaller files mean faster page loading — especially important on mobile devices with slower connections. Additional techniques like loading="lazy" and fetchpriority="high" speed up rendering.

Tools like PageSpeed Insights and Lighthouse identify specific files worth optimizing.

contentBlocks.title: Tips for converting JPG to PNG
contentBlocks.description: A few tips to help you avoid common issues during conversion:

items.title: Very large files
items.description: Converting images above 4000×4000 pixels may be slower and strain the browser. If processing many large files, consider splitting them into batches of 10–20.

items.title: Already compressed files
items.description: If the original JPG file was already heavily compressed, converting to PNG may not yield significant size savings. Try different quality settings.

items.title: Transparency
items.description: The PNG format supports alpha channel (transparency). If the original JPG file contains transparent areas, they will be preserved.

items.title: Keep your originals
items.description: Lossy conversion is irreversible. Keep copies of original JPG files so you can re-convert with different settings if needed.

contentBlocks.title: How much can you save by converting JPG to PNG?
contentBlocks.html:
Savings depend on the source file type and its original compression. Below are example results:

Camera photo

2.4 MB → 890 KB

Saving: ~63%

Product image

500 KB → 185 KB

Saving: ~63%

Screenshot / banner

350 KB → 230 KB

Saving: ~34%

Actual savings may vary depending on image content and quality settings. The converter shows the exact size before and after conversion for each file.

contentBlocks.title: Frequently asked questions about JPG to PNG conversion

items.question: Does converting JPG to PNG improve image quality?
items.answer: No. PNG is lossless, but it cannot restore detail already discarded by JPG compression. The resulting PNG is a pixel-perfect copy of the JPG — just stored in a lossless container.

items.question: Why is the PNG file larger than the original JPG?
items.answer: PNG uses lossless compression, which preserves every pixel. JPG achieves smaller sizes by discarding data. So the same image in PNG will almost always be larger.

items.question: Can I convert multiple files at once?
items.answer: Yes. There is no file limit. Add as many JPGs as you like and convert them all in a single batch.

items.question: Are my files uploaded to a server?
items.answer: No. Everything runs locally in your browser. Files never leave your device.

items.question: Will the converted PNG have transparency?
items.answer: No. JPG does not contain transparency data, so the PNG will have a solid (white) background. You can add transparency later using a graphics editor.

items.question: Does this tool work on mobile?
items.answer: Yes, but we recommend a laptop or desktop for the best experience. The converter uses the HTML5 Canvas API supported by all modern browsers.

items.question: What is the maximum file size?
items.answer: Individual files up to 100 MB are supported. There is no cap on the total number of files per session.

items.question: Can I convert PNG back to JPG?
items.answer: Absolutely. Use our PNG to JPG converter to switch back at any time.
